title: Project scaffolding
analysis: Task 1 is project scaffolding: create pyproject.toml with all PRD dependencies, config.yaml template matching the PRD configuration section exactly, and the full directory structure with __init__.py files. This is a greenfield project — the repo currently only has CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, orchestrator-config.yaml, docs/prd-autopilot-editor.md, and task infrastructure. The PRD specifies a detailed project structure (autopilot/ with 7 subpackages + prompts dir), tests/fixtures/, scripts/, and output/. The config.yaml must have: input_dir, output_dir, creator profile, cameras, output targets, models, llm, youtube, processing sections. pyproject.toml must use uv/hatch build system with Python 3.11+. There is a chicken-and-egg issue: we need pyproject.toml to run pytest, but we want to TDD the pyproject.toml contents. Solution: prerequisite creates a minimal pyproject.toml + tests dir so pytest can execute, then TDD steps verify and expand from there.

prerequisites.description: Create minimal pyproject.toml with hatchling build system, Python >=3.11, and pytest as dev dependency so we can run tests. Create tests/__init__.py and tests/conftest.py with a helper that resolves project root.

steps.description: Write tests/test_scaffolding.py with test_directory_structure_exists() that asserts all required directories exist (autopilot/, autopilot/ingest/, autopilot/analyze/, autopilot/organize/, autopilot/plan/, autopilot/source/, autopilot/render/, autopilot/upload/, autopilot/prompts/, tests/fixtures/, scripts/, output/) and that each Python package has an __init__.py file (autopilot/ and its 7 subpackages). Also assert prompts/ does NOT have __init__.py (it's not a Python package).

Create all directories and __init__.py files: autopilot/__init__.py (with __version__ = '0.1.0'), autopilot/ingest/__init__.py, autopilot/analyze/__init__.py, autopilot/organize/__init__.py, autopilot/plan/__init__.py, autopilot/source/__init__.py, autopilot/render/__init__.py, autopilot/upload/__init__.py, autopilot/prompts/ (empty dir with .gitkeep), tests/fixtures/ (empty dir with .gitkeep), scripts/ (empty dir with .gitkeep), output/ (empty dir with .gitkeep). Verify test passes.

Write test_pyproject_toml_valid() that: (1) parses pyproject.toml as valid TOML, (2) asserts build-system requires hatchling, (3) asserts project.requires-python >= '3.11', (4) asserts project.name == 'autopilot-video', (5) asserts all required dependency groups exist. Write test_pyproject_toml_has_all_dependencies() that checks the dependencies list contains all required packages: click, pyyaml, whisperx, faster-whisper, ultralytics, insightface, transformers, faiss-gpu, panns-inference, audiocraft, scikit-learn, moviepy, pyav, opentimelineio, scenedetect, kokoro, anthropic, google-api-python-client, requests. Write test_pyproject_toml_has_dev_dependencies() checking pytest, ruff, pyright are in dev optional-dependencies.

Update pyproject.toml with all dependencies from the PRD technology stack. Use hatchling build backend. Set name='autopilot-video', version from autopilot/__init__.py, requires-python='>=3.11'. Group deps: core runtime (click, pyyaml, requests), ML (whisperx, faster-whisper, ultralytics, insightface, transformers, faiss-gpu, panns-inference, audiocraft, scikit-learn), video (moviepy>=2.0, pyav, opentimelineio, scenedetect), TTS (kokoro), API (anthropic, google-api-python-client). Dev deps as optional [dev]: pytest, ruff, pyright. Add [tool.pytest.ini_options] with testpaths=['tests']. Add [tool.ruff] config. Add [tool.pyright] config. Verify test passes.

Write test_config_yaml_valid() that: (1) parses config.yaml as valid YAML, (2) asserts all required top-level keys exist: input_dir, output_dir, creator, cameras, output, models, llm, youtube, processing. Write test_config_yaml_creator_section() checking creator has keys: name, channel_style, target_audience, default_video_duration_minutes, narration_style, music_preference. Write test_config_yaml_models_section() checking models has: whisper_size, yolo_variant, yolo_sample_every_n_frames, clip_model, face_model, tts_engine, music_engine. Write test_config_yaml_processing_section() checking processing has: max_wall_clock_hours, gpu_device, num_cpu_workers, batch_size_yolo, batch_size_whisper. Write test_config_yaml_output_section() checking output has: primary_aspect, resolution, codec, quality_crf, audio_bitrate, target_loudness_lufs. Write test_config_yaml_llm_section() checking llm has: provider, planning_model, utility_model. Write test_config_yaml_youtube_section() checking youtube has: privacy_status, default_category, credentials_path.

Create config.yaml matching the PRD configuration section exactly. Include all sections with the exact default values from the PRD: input_dir (placeholder ~/footage/), output_dir (placeholder ~/output/), creator profile, camera profiles (dji_osmo_action_6 with source_resolution, aspect_mode, has_gyro_data, default_crop_target, crop_smoothing_tau), output targets, model preferences, LLM config (anthropic provider, claude-opus-4-20250514 planning, claude-sonnet-4-20250514 utility), YouTube config, processing config. Verify test passes.

Write test_gitignore_has_output_dir() that verifies .gitignore contains 'output/' to prevent committing rendered videos. Write test_package_version() that imports autopilot and checks __version__ is a valid semver string.

Update .gitignore to include output/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .eggs/, *.egg-info/, dist/, build/, .venv/, .env, *.credentials.json entries. Ensure autopilot/__init__.py has __version__ = '0.1.0'. Verify all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use hatchling as build backend (not setuptools)
design_decisions.rationale: The task specifies 'uv/hatch build system'. Hatchling is the modern Python build backend that pairs with uv for dependency management. It supports dynamic version reading from __init__.py.

design_decisions.decision: Put __version__ in autopilot/__init__.py, read dynamically in pyproject.toml
design_decisions.rationale: Single source of truth for version. Hatchling supports [tool.hatch.version] with path-based version reading. This is the standard hatch pattern.
The prompts directory holds .md template files, not Python modules. It should not be a Python package. .gitkeep ensures git tracks the empty directory.

design_decisions.decision: Dev dependencies go in [project.optional-dependencies] dev group, not separate requirements file
design_decisions.rationale: Modern Python packaging puts dev deps in optional-dependencies. Installable via 'uv pip install -e .[dev]' or 'pip install -e .[dev]'. Keeps everything in one file.

design_decisions.decision: Use tomllib (stdlib in 3.11+) to parse pyproject.toml in tests
design_decisions.rationale: Python 3.11+ includes tomllib in stdlib, so no extra test dependency needed. This validates the TOML is well-formed programmatically.

design_decisions.decision: Prerequisite creates minimal pyproject.toml before TDD steps
design_decisions.rationale: Chicken-and-egg: pytest needs pyproject.toml to discover tests. The prerequisite creates just enough to run pytest (project name, python version, pytest dep), then step-3/step-4 TDD the full dependency list.

design_decisions.decision: Config values match PRD defaults exactly, not placeholders
design_decisions.rationale: The task says 'matching the PRD configuration section exactly'. We use the exact values from the PRD (e.g., CRF 18, LUFS -16, claude-opus-4-20250514) so downstream tasks can rely on these defaults.

Git doesn't track empty directories. .gitkeep is the convention for ensuring empty directories are committed. output/ will later hold rendered videos (gitignored), but the directory should exist in the repo.

reuse.how: Copy the exact YAML from the PRD as the config.yaml template, with all sections and default values preserved.
Use the exact directory layout specified in the PRD to create the project skeleton. The 7 subpackages under autopilot/ and the support directories match the PRD exactly.
Map the 'Key Python Libraries' table directly to pyproject.toml dependencies. Every library listed becomes a dependency entry.
The test_command (pytest), lint_command (ruff check), and type_check_command (pyright) confirm the dev tooling choices. Configure these in pyproject.toml [tool.*] sections.
